data: TATA Group offers career opportunities across its subsidiaries including Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), Tata Steel, Tata Motors, Tata Power, and Tata Communications. Each subsidiary has its own hiring process and career page.

TCS primarily hires through campus placements, national-level exams like TCS NQT, and lateral hiring portals. The selection process typically includes an aptitude test, technical interview, and HR round.

TATA offers internships for undergraduate and postgraduate students across disciplines like engineering, business, and design. Interns are assigned mentors and work on real-world projects for 8–12 weeks during summer or winter terms.

TCS National Qualifier Test (NQT) is an entry-level assessment used to screen candidates for multiple roles within TCS and partner companies. It tests aptitude, reasoning, programming logic, and English proficiency.
Tata Communications offers roles in network engineering, cloud solutions, cybersecurity, sales, and project management. Job seekers can apply through the career portal or third-party job platforms.

Tata Motors runs graduate programs for mechanical, automotive, and electrical engineering students. These include on-the-job training, rotation through departments, and mentorship from senior professionals.
TATA employees enjoy benefits such as health insurance, flexible working hours, skill development programs, paid parental leave, and access to TATA-wide internal mobility opportunities.
TATA companies also conduct off-campus drives through online platforms and coding contests. Eligibility often includes recent graduates with consistent academic performance and no standing backlogs.

Many interns at Tata Steel and Tata Elxsi have reported positive experiences, citing meaningful projects, a collaborative culture, and good exposure to industry challenges during their internship period.
TCS offers structured career progression through role-based promotions, certifications, internal job postings (IJP), and leadership development tracks like Elevate and Xplore.

As of 2024, TATA companies are expanding hiring in areas like AI, electric vehicles, renewable energy, and cybersecurity. They are also strengthening campus engagement and increasing internship conversion rates.
